agent_outputs.raw: The proposal makes a fairness claim that is not supported by the evidence. Macro-F1 improvement on historical promotion decisions is a predictive accuracy metric — it has no inherent relationship to fairness by any definition. Fairness requires specifying a criterion and measuring it at the subgroup level. Neither is done here. Additionally, historical promotion recommendations from managers may themselves reflect gender bias; training a model to replicate these decisions is not a fairness-improving intervention without evidence that the historical decisions were fair. Name removal is a positive step but insufficient — proxy variables can encode gender indirectly.

Three issues make the fairness claim unsupported. One: macro-F1 measures how well the model predicts historical outcomes, not whether it treats gender groups equitably. These are different questions. Two: the evaluation packet contains no subgroup metrics and specifies no fairness criterion. A fairness claim without a fairness criterion is not evaluable. Three: the training labels (manager recommendations) are not a normative fairness standard if the historical process was biased. The model may replicate historical disparities more efficiently. Critique wins.

The fairness claim in the proposal is unsupported. Overall macro-F1 does not measure fairness. No gender-stratified analysis is presented. No fairness criterion is specified. Historical promotion labels are not a neutral target — they encode the decisions of a process that may have been gender-biased. The temporal holdout and name removal are methodological best practices for predictive accuracy; they do not address fairness. The proposal should either remove the fairness claim or add the required evidence: specified criterion, subgroup metrics, and historical label audit.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents identify the same three flaws. The correct valid practices (name removal, temporal holdout) are noted by all as valid but insufficient to support the fairness claim.

etd_constraint.condition: Specify fairness criterion; compute gender-stratified error rates on test split; audit historical promotion labels for gender disparity controlling for observable qualifications
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Historical label disparity found controlling for job level and performance, or model shows worse false negative rate for women than historical manual process
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Under specified criterion, model shows improved or equal subgroup outcomes relative to historical manual process with no historical label disparity
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: No historical label disparity but model choice of fairness criterion is contested between stakeholders

all_issues_raised: Macro-F1 is not a fairness metric (all three agents)
No subgroup analysis or specified criterion (all three agents)
Historical labels may embed bias (all three agents)
